---
title: "Western Patagonia Hydrology & Climate Change"
status: completed
start: 2019
---


# Western Patagonia: Freshwater Sources Under Climate Change

**Status:** Completed (2019–2023)  
**Institution:** Universidad de Concepción, Chile  
**Supervisors:** Mauricio Aguayo, Jorge León

## Summary

This PhD research assessed the impacts of climate change on the spatio-temporal patterns of freshwater sources to the coastal system of Western Patagonia (40–56°S). Using a combination of hydrological models, glacier projections, and ensemble climate scenarios, the study quantified future changes in runoff regimes and their implications for coastal ecosystems.

## Methods

- Glacio-hydrological modelling (HBV, TETIS, OGGM)
- CMIP5/CMIP6 multi-model ensembles
- Uncertainty partitioning (climate scenarios, model parameters, glacier response)
- Remote sensing for snow and glacier monitoring

## Datasets

- PatagoniaMet (published in *Scientific Data*, 2024)
- Glacier runoff projections ensemble (Zenodo, 2024)

## Related Publications

- Aguayo et al. (2025), *Earth's Future* — hybrid glacio-hydrological modelling
- Aguayo et al. (2024), *The Cryosphere* — uncertainty in glacier runoff projections
- Aguayo et al. (2024), *Scientific Data* — PatagoniaMet dataset
- Aguayo et al. (2021), *Scientific Reports* — hydrological droughts
- Aguayo et al. (2019), *Climatic Change* — freshwater input to coastal system

## Code

- [GitHub](https://github.com/rodaguayo)
